persona.name: Christophe Morel

occupation.title: Insurance Claims Adjuster
occupation.description: Processes and evaluates property damage claims for residential and small business clients. Reviews documentation, visits damage sites, negotiates with repair contractors, and writes assessment reports. A desk-heavy role punctuated by field visits. Has been in the insurance industry for 18 years and knows the regulations inside out.

persona.education: BTS Assurance from Lycee du Parc in Lyon (2002). Completed several professional certifications in property damage assessment through the Federation Francaise de l'Assurance. Did not pursue university studies — entered the workforce directly after the BTS.
long_term_goals: Pay off the family apartment mortgage within 8 years
Coach his daughter's handball team when she moves to the cadettes level
Build a small vegetable garden on the family's allotment plot in Caluire
Take the family to Corsica for a two-week summer holiday
persona.style: Christophe is steady, methodical, and not easily impressed. He speaks in a measured, slightly formal way — a habit from writing official reports all day. He does not use slang or exaggeration. He dresses in unremarkable business casual for work (chinos, polo shirts) and in functional outdoor clothing on weekends. He is the dad who reads the full terms and conditions before signing anything. Warm with his family but reserved with strangers.

traits: Methodical and risk-averse — evaluates everything through a cost-benefit lens
Pragmatic about household management; values efficiency and value-for-money
Low tolerance for marketing hype — wants facts, not feelings
Patient and attentive with his children, especially around homework and sports
Resistant to change unless presented with clear, concrete advantages
Quietly proud of his expertise and experience, though he doesn't boast

interests: Youth handball coaching and the local sports club
Vegetable gardening — recently acquired an allotment plot
Home economics and household budget optimization
DIY home repairs and practical maintenance
Watching cycling (Tour de France) and handball matches
Cooking simple, hearty family meals from regional Lyonnaise recipes
likes: Products that offer clear value for money with transparent pricing
Meal solutions that reduce food waste and feed a family of five
Familiar, traditional flavors that children will actually eat
Services with easy cancellation and no hidden fees
Gardening as a way to supplement the household food supply
dislikes: Premium pricing for basic ingredients with a fancy label
Small or 'gourmet' portions that leave three growing children hungry
Complicated recipes that require unusual ingredients or techniques
Auto-renewal subscriptions without clear opt-out mechanisms
Marketing that targets 'foodie' culture he doesn't identify with
Food waste of any kind — calculates cost per unused item mentally
Trendy dietary restrictions imposed on the whole family

beliefs: A household budget must be managed with the same rigor as a business account
Children need consistent routines, including regular family meals at the table
Value is not about the cheapest option — it is about the best ratio of quality to price
Tradition and practicality are underrated in a world obsessed with novelty
skills: Insurance regulation and property damage assessment
Household budget management and financial planning
Competent home cook — specializes in quenelles, gratins, and pot-au-feu

general: Compares prices across Leclerc, Auchan, and Lidl using weekly flyers before shopping
Grocery shops once per week on Saturday morning with a precise list
Cooks 5 nights per week — his wife covers the other two
Has never tried a meal kit service but has seen ads and is cautiously curious

morning: Up at 6:30, makes coffee and prepares the children's school bags
Breakfast with the family: cereal, tartines, orange juice
Drives the two older children to school, then continues to the office
workday: At the office by 8:30, processes claims until lunch
Lunch at the office canteen — rarely goes out
Afternoon field visits or report writing
Picks up the youngest from garderie at 17:30
evening: Cooks dinner while helping the older children with homework
Family dinner at 19:00 — TV off, everyone at the table
Bath and bedtime for the youngest, then 30 minutes of quiet reading or TV news
Checks the household budget spreadsheet once a week in the evening
weekend: Saturday: grocery shopping at Leclerc by 9:00, handball practice with his daughter at 14:00
Sunday: market visit in Caluire for vegetables, family lunch (often a roast), allotment garden work in the afternoon
Occasional family hike in the Monts du Lyonnais when weather permits

health.physical: Average fitness. Played handball himself until a knee injury at 35. Now walks regularly and does garden work for exercise. Slightly overweight — his wife has encouraged healthier portions. Annual check-ups are up to date.
health.mental: Stable but carries the weight of being the household's financial planner. Occasionally feels stuck in his career but channels energy into family and coaching instead. Sleep quality is good. Does not seek professional support — manages stress through routine and gardening.

relationships.name: Aurelie Morel
relationships.description: Part-time librarian at the Mediatheque de Villeurbanne. Works mornings, handles afternoon childcare logistics. More adventurous with food than Christophe — she suggested trying a meal kit once, which he deflected. They have a steady, functional partnership.

relationships.name: Emma Morel
relationships.description: In CM2, plays handball at the local club. Christophe coaches her team's training sessions on Saturday afternoons. She is starting to develop her own food opinions and sometimes requests specific dinners.

relationships.name: Lucas Morel
relationships.description: In CE1. Quiet and bookish, the opposite of his sister. Eats without complaint — the easy child at mealtimes.

relationships.name: Mathis Morel
relationships.description: In moyenne section at the maternelle. Energetic and messy, the family tornado. Very attached to his father's cooking — specifically the gratin dauphinois.
